epic.name: Database & Models
epic.objective: Implement PostgreSQL database layer with GORM, migrations, and core data models (Alert, Incident, TimelineEntry)
epic.definition_of_done: All v0.1 models exist with migrations, immutable timestamp constraints enforced, and repository layer provides CRUD operations

tasks.name: Set up GORM with PostgreSQL connection
tasks.description: Configure GORM with PostgreSQL driver, connection pooling, and structured logging
acceptance_criteria: Database connection established from DATABASE_URL env var
Connection pool configured (max open, max idle, max lifetime)
GORM logger outputs structured JSON
Graceful connection handling on shutdown
Connection retry logic on startup

tasks.name: Implement database migration system
tasks.description: Set up golang-migrate for versioned schema migrations with up/down support
acceptance_criteria: migrations/ directory with numbered migration files
make migrate applies pending migrations
make migrate-down rolls back last migration
Migration status can be checked
Migrations run automatically on application start in development

tasks.name: Create Alert model and migration
tasks.description: Implement Alert struct and database schema as defined in architecture.md with immutable received_at timestamp
acceptance_criteria: Alert struct with all fields: id (UUID), external_id, source, fingerprint, status, severity, title, description, labels (JSONB), annotations (JSONB), raw_payload (JSONB), started_at, ended_at, received_at, created_at
Migration creates alerts table with proper types
UNIQUE constraint on (source, external_id)
received_at defaults to NOW() and is immutable
Appropriate indexes on source, external_id, status, severity, received_at

tasks.name: Create Incident model and migration
tasks.description: Implement Incident struct and database schema as defined in architecture.md with sequential incident_number and immutable timestamps
acceptance_criteria: Incident struct with all fields: id (UUID), incident_number (SERIAL), title, slug, status, severity, summary, slack_channel_id, slack_channel_name, created_at, triggered_at, acknowledged_at, resolved_at, created_by_type, created_by_id
Migration creates incidents table with proper types
incident_number is auto-incrementing SERIAL
created_at and triggered_at default to NOW() and are immutable
Status enum constraint: triggered, acknowledged, resolved, canceled
Severity enum constraint: critical, high, medium, low
Indexes on status, severity, created_at, incident_number

tasks.name: Create IncidentAlert join table and migration
tasks.description: Implement many-to-many relationship between incidents and alerts
acceptance_criteria: incident_alerts table with incident_id, alert_id, linked_at, linked_by_type, linked_by_id
Composite primary key on (incident_id, alert_id)
Foreign key constraints with CASCADE delete
linked_at defaults to NOW()

tasks.name: Create TimelineEntry model and migration
tasks.description: Implement immutable timeline entry model with database trigger preventing updates as per ADR-003
acceptance_criteria: TimelineEntry struct with: id (UUID), incident_id, timestamp, type, actor_type, actor_id, content (JSONB), created_at
Migration creates timeline_entries table
timestamp and created_at default to NOW() and are immutable
Database trigger prevents UPDATE operations on timeline_entries
Database trigger prevents DELETE operations on timeline_entries
Foreign key to incidents with CASCADE delete
Index on (incident_id, timestamp)

tasks.name: Implement Alert repository
tasks.description: Create repository layer for Alert CRUD operations with proper error handling
acceptance_criteria: Create(alert) - inserts new alert
GetByID(id) - retrieves alert by UUID
GetByExternalID(source, externalID) - retrieves by unique constraint
List(filters, pagination) - lists alerts with filtering
Update(alert) - updates mutable fields only
All methods return typed errors for not found, duplicate, etc.

tasks.name: Implement Incident repository
tasks.description: Create repository layer for Incident CRUD operations
acceptance_criteria: Create(incident) - inserts new incident, returns with generated incident_number
GetByID(id) - retrieves incident by UUID
GetByNumber(number) - retrieves by incident_number
List(filters, pagination) - lists with status/severity/date filters
Update(incident) - updates mutable fields, sets acknowledged_at/resolved_at on status change
LinkAlert(incidentID, alertID) - creates incident_alert record
GetAlerts(incidentID) - retrieves linked alerts

tasks.name: Implement TimelineEntry repository
acceptance_criteria: Create(entry) - inserts new timeline entry
GetByIncidentID(incidentID, pagination) - lists entries for incident ordered by timestamp
No Update or Delete methods exist
Bulk create for importing multiple entries
